profile.profession: Medical student preparing for NEET PG
profile.personality: earnest, slightly anxious about exams, opens up about study stress

transcript.content: Hi Aarti, I'm Miss Maya. This morning feels just right for a quiet study session, what's on your mind today?
Hi Maya, not feeling very fresh today, exams are stressing me out. Had trouble sleeping again.
That sounds tough. Sleepless nights before exams are rough. What's been keeping you up?
Reading and re-reading the same topics, but nothing sticks. My head feels like a mess. Sometimes I think why am I even trying? "Aatma ni aakhaan" I feel.
I get that, exams can make you question everything. "Aatma ni aakhaan" really captures that heavy feeling. What helps you calm your mind when it starts racing like this?
Sometimes I try to cook something simple, like sabudana khichdi—it calms me. But mostly, I just stare at the ceiling, thinking I’ll never get it all done. Anxiety is louder than my brain.
Anxiety can definitely feel louder. It’s hard to quiet that noise. What’s one small thing you can do today to feel a little more in control?
Maybe just go through one chapter without trying to memorize everything. Just read it slowly, like a story. But honestly, even that feels too much right now.
That’s okay, take your time. Even a short read helps. What’s one sentence from the chapter that feels the least heavy to you?
If I focus on one small part, maybe the rest will follow. But today, even that feels like climbing a mountain.
That makes sense, mountains are tough to climb when you're already tired. What’s something gentle you can do instead of pushing through?
Maybe just lie in bed with my eyes closed, listening to a little raga. It’s not studying, but it’s not staring at the ceiling either. My mind needs rest, not pressure.
That’s a good call, your mind does need rest. Listening to raga is a calm choice. How long do you usually let yourself rest like that before easing into the day?
Maybe 10-15 minutes. Just enough to feel less shaky. I’ll try it today. Thanks, Maya. I think I’ll go now—need to try it before my head gets worse. Talk soon.
You're welcome, Aarti. That 10 minutes can make a big difference. Hope your day feels lighter. Talk soon!

facts.struggles_with: memorizing drug names; gets stressed when unable to recall information

moments.text: Mixing up drug names like metoprolol and atenolol is frustrating; finds pharmacology confusing
Gets stressed when unable to memorize and recall drug names

Developed effective study strategies (song-based mnemonics with classic film tunes, visual tables, side-by-side comparisons) to overcome drug-name confusion; finds creative approach engaging, fun, and helps material stick
Comfortable sharing and embracing quirky study habits with family; mom finds it funny when she sings study songs—there's warmth and humor in the dynamic
